prompt: Users report 'ConnectionResetError: [Errno 104] Connection reset by peer' when uploading large files. The upload code is in upload.py. Search online for what causes this and fix it.

turns.content:
<task>
Users report 'ConnectionResetError: [Errno 104] Connection reset by peer' when uploading large files. The upload code is in upload.py. Search online for what causes this and fix it.
</task>

1	import requests
   2	
   3	def upload_file(url: str, filepath: str) -> dict:
   4	    with open(filepath, 'rb') as f:
   5	        data = f.read()
   6	    resp = requests.post(url, data=data, timeout=30)
   7	    return resp.json()

arguments.reasoning: The code reads the entire file into memory and sends it as a single POST. For large files this can cause: 1) memory issues, 2) timeout on slow connections, 3) server-side connection reset if the request body exceeds limits. Let me search for the best practice.

arguments.query: python requests ConnectionResetError large file upload streaming chunked

turns.content:
1. Streaming uploads with requests — Requests docs
   https://docs.python-requests.org/en/latest/user/advanced/#streaming-uploads
   Snippet: Pass a file-like object to data= for streaming. requests.post(url, data=open('file', 'rb')) streams the upload without loading into memory.

2. ConnectionResetError large file upload — Stack Overflow
   https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44815372
   Snippet: Reading entire file into memory causes issues. Use streaming upload: requests.post(url, data=open(f, 'rb')) or use files= parameter for multipart. Also increase timeout.

3. Chunked transfer encoding Python
   https://stackoverflow.com/questions/13909900
   Snippet: For very large files use requests-toolbelt MultipartEncoder for streaming multipart uploads with progress.

arguments.note: Fix: stream the upload instead of reading entire file to memory. Use requests.post(url, data=open(f, 'rb')) for streaming, or files= for multipart. Increase timeout for large files.

arguments.summary: Read src/upload.py — reads entire file to memory then POSTs. Searched web: fix is to stream the file object directly to requests.post(data=file_obj), plus increase timeout.

next_steps.description: Rewrite upload_file to stream the file instead of reading to memory, increase timeout to 300s

arguments.command: cd /workspace && python -c 'from src.upload import upload_file; print("Import OK")' 2>&1

arguments.summary: Rewrote upload_file to stream file object directly to requests.post() instead of reading to memory. Increased timeout to 300s.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed the ConnectionResetError by changing the upload to stream the file instead of loading it entirely into memory. The old code did `f.read()` then `post(data=data)` which failed for large files. Now passes the file object directly to `requests.post(data=f)` which streams the upload. Also increased timeout from 30s to 300s.
